title: Chapter 307 – Hyeon Won (2)
content:
"As an official, I'm rather curious. As a martial artist, I mean... My qualifications were also considered a rare martial talent, at least where I come from."

The scent of fine tea drifted through the guest room with its smoothly polished wooden furnishings.

Sunlight flowed in like rippling water, casting pools of white light beneath the window frames, while the aroma of precious dragon well tea wafted with refined elegance.

"You said you're seventeen. I once had a question while hearing tales of the first Heavenly Demon and the Three Peak Immortal of Wudang Mountain, and I believe it applies to you as well. How did you become so formidable at such a young age?"

[TL Note- Three Peak Immortal is a name for Zhang Sanfeng, the founder of Wudang Sect.]

"Just happened that way..."

"Yes, it defies explanation—which is precisely why someone like you stands before me now. But I was truly stunned when Zhuge Clan Leader's protective energy shattered. My energy sense is quite refined, and when I observed carefully, your energy release was exquisite and terrifying in equal measure. It seemed to seep into the gaps in Zhuge's internal energy barrier like puzzle pieces sliding into place. It happened so quickly that I'm not certain I perceived it correctly..."

The air enveloping the inner chamber was remarkably tranquil.

The peculiar expressions on the faces of the Desolate Fortress black-ranks seated around the round table matched that of Deputy Commander Kang Su-hui as he lavished praise upon his subordinate.

The Deputy Commander spoke strictly from fact, weaving in his own insights and experiences.

His manner of speech blurred the line between sincere admiration and calculated flattery, complemented by a voice that carried deep resonance.

A high-ranking official of the Beijing imperial court.

He inhabited an entirely different world.

Jeong Yeon-shin nodded somewhat absently while thinking of Hyeon Won-chang.

If he had gone to the Forbidden City instead of Desolate Fortress, wouldn't he have achieved even greater success? No, that wasn't right. Then there would be no Radiant Demon Wing, no Lightning Genius.

Perhaps it was the pain afflicting him both internally and externally, but all manner of stray thoughts crossed his mind.

"Have I gone too far with my rambling?"

Deputy Commander Kang Su-hui, who had been quietly observing Radiant Demon Wing Leader, smiled faintly.

His smile carried a cunning that belied his appearance of being in his thirties. His actual age must be considerably advanced.

"I plan to kill all the aristocrats gathered here. I intend to eradicate the martial lineages of Shanxi. Since my first approach was thwarted, I must proceed with the second."

The declaration came without warning.

This was his true purpose.

Jeong Yeon-shin's collar, which had been gradually lowering with his breath, froze mid-descent.

The clothing he currently wore was not the formal attire of a Desolate Fortress black-rank. His uncle's long robe had been reduced to tatters by the Yin and Yang Divine Whip.

Because of this, he had received a plain black robe from the Gongya Family.

It bore no "Desolate" character. The family's tailor had fled during the battle, and Young Family Head Gongya Jeong had personally apologized for the omission.

Jeong Yeon-shin didn't mind.

He had another set of clothes besides the one he received from his uncle, but the "Desolate" character was too large, making him reluctant to wear it.

It inevitably reminded him of Hyeon Won-chang.

'Eradicate the martial lineages of Shanxi?'

Glancing left and right, he found that his two seniors' expressions remained unchanged.

Yue Shou-lin appeared to be forcing sternness onto her alcohol-loosened lips, while Jin Myeong-jo maintained his usual slightly downcast eyes and cool composure.

Jeong Yeon-shin decided to hold his silence for the moment.

"Martial artists are fundamentally of no benefit to the world. They create nothing—only exploit and destroy. They are nothing more than beasts upon the land who don't even pay taxes... treating commoners as mere backdrops or kindling."

He growled with a human voice but the intensity of a beast. The authority of the highest military official poured from him.

"I once had an attendant who returned to his hometown after serving me. A child of innate goodness despite his lack of education. He would beam with delight whenever I paid him generously. I can still see his face clearly. He said Shanxi was his hometown... He opened an inn but died ten years ago, caught in a skirmish between martial artists. Yes, he's dead and gone."

As the Deputy Commander continued, his expression grew increasingly glacial. He seemed to be immersing himself in his own narrative.

"Martial artists."

As he spoke of the martial world's darker face from an outsider's perspective, his fingers tapping the table gradually became more forceful.

"Aren't they the primary culprits in disrupting the nation's peace? Even more so during famines. Rather than contributing to the harvest, they wield their martial arts selfishly to fill their festering bellies, claiming they cannot be allowed to starve. They plunder while posing as the strong. Before being martial artists, they are humans. In a world where killers with martial skills run rampant without law... it's truly inevitable."

The Deputy Commander muttered as if chewing each word before swallowing it.

"The martial world is stuffed with romantic garbage. Only a tiny minority of righteous heroes shine, but that constellation is so small and dim it doesn't even register in my eyes. I plan to erase it with this opportunity. I've spent ten years preparing to annihilate the Shanxi martial world. I've thoroughly investigated the unsolved cases they left behind in their careless self-preservation."

Swish.

He suddenly produced an elaborately decorated scroll with a greenish-yellow hue from his bosom.

It was an unusually thick document, which he immediately extended toward Jeong Yeon-shin.

"I swear that every word written here is true. On my family name, my position, and my honor. Though it may seem insignificant, it represents the result of thorough investigation conducted alongside the Prefects, Surveillance Commissioners, and Regional Commanders of Shanxi. The Provincial Governor of Shanxi is my sister."

He seemed to disregard the black-rank seniority hierarchy, since it was Jeong Yeon-shin who had slain Zhuge Clan Leader.

"......"

Jeong Yeon-shin hesitated to accept it. A request from the Deputy Commander would likely be calculated as a merit exceeding even a black-rank mission.

Accepting it outright would be disrespectful to Jin Myeong-jo and Yue Shou-lin.

What should I do? His thoughts became consumed with the conflict. The Luminous Wheel in his heart inevitably loosened somewhat.

It was then.

Suddenly, a pitch-black sleeve cuff brushed over the end of the scroll. The movement carried a trace of energy.

The document rolled toward Jeong Yeon-shin. It came with Jin Myeong-jo's ice-cold voice.

"You beheaded Zhuge Liang. Don't hesitate to step forward."

After offering this counsel, he rose from his seat. He said he would retire due to his condition.

Flutter—

The pitch-black of his long robe embodied Divine Blood Extreme Demon's silhouette. It seemed profoundly solitary... Until he opened the door and departed, his footsteps were remarkably natural. Jeong Yeon-shin's heart swelled slightly as he witnessed his senior's caliber.

"I'll just watch our Lightning Genius."

Yue Shou-lin snickered in her intoxicated state, tilting her head sideways.

The pain from her injuries must rival having muscles torn from bone, yet she was smiling.

Jeong Yeon-shin couldn't bring himself to tell her to dispel her intoxication with her major meridian.

Slowly unrolling the document, he revealed a report written in classical style.

[Great Lake Hui Family.

They are a group based in Xin Prefecture's Wuliang Mountain estate who engage in plunder.

They are proficient in both sword and fist techniques, with particularly strong grip strength.

There are rumors that they resort to cannibalism when unable to procure food from villages, and this was confirmed after tracking the activities of family head Hui Hu-liang all the way to Taiyuan Prefecture.

Although suspicions have been raised about them before, the family head dispelled unfavorable scrutiny by killing Blood Flame Cult swordsmen who were roaming near Xin Prefecture.

With a long-established estate, they hold considerable influence in the Shanxi martial world.]

[Zhiqiao Martial Academy.

They are a group in Shanxi's Ze Prefecture who accept silver to train disciples.

Adult disciples regularly disappear, but the academy claims they have been sent to Beijing to take the military examination.

The academy masters possess formidable martial arts and high prestige. They maintain order in the villages by protecting them from violent wanderers, and their reputation in the area is positive.

This year, four sons of Taiyuan Prefecture merchants and three daughters of Pingyang Prefecture landowners who were accepted as disciples have vanished.

None of them have been confirmed in Beijing.

It is suspected they are involved in human trafficking connected to Heaven Conquering Sect, one of the Thirteen Evil Sects' Four Great Faction.

Evidence is attached in the enclosed document. Among the "surviving disciples" of Heaven Conquering Sect, there is one from Shanxi.

Those who fail to become disciples are believed to be eliminated.

They originally targeted children from humble families who spent most of their wealth on entrance fees, but due to the famine, they appear to have become less discriminating about their disciples' backgrounds.

It is presumed they attended the Shanxi martial arts tournament to recruit those who failed to enter Zhuge Clan Leader's tutelage.]

Rustle, rustle.

Jeong Yeon-shin turned the pages one by one. The reports were generally similar in substance.

They detailed the appearances, martial arts, and crimes of martial artists who had established power. The back pages included evidence such as illustrations or ledgers.

"There is work to be done, I see."

Jeong Yeon-shin murmured slowly.

"I know you need time to recover, but please help me. A day like today—when these villains gather in one place—will not come again. However, if we provoke them carelessly and they scatter using their lightness skills, we'll only end up swelling the ranks of bandits in the green woods. We need overwhelming lightness skill mobility and formidable martial prowess."

The Deputy Commander's voice accompanied him.

"I have no intention of offering compensation that might tarnish your reputation. Just hear me out. I seem to have already received your answer."

"......"

"When the time comes for a purple-rank position to be decided at Desolate Fortress, His Majesty and the Desolate Fortress Lord exchange messages through spirit beasts. It is an extremely sacred discussion. During this process, His Majesty tends to seek the opinion of his highest officials once—regarding how well the candidate's character and martial temperament align with the nation's affairs. As someone who has made contact with you, I will certainly be consulted. Well, this seems unnecessary for you, though..."

"I've read everything. These individuals have ties to local dignitaries, so how will you handle the backlash when they're eliminated?"

Jeong Yeon-shin asked as he rolled up the document and handed it back.

Deputy Commander Kang Su-hui's lips curved into a smooth arc.

"I have more evidence. I plan to display it when I impose additional punishment on their corpses for all to see."

***

The Gongya Family Head still hadn't regained consciousness.

It was because he had been severely beaten by the now-deceased Zhuge Clan Leader. That he hadn't died instantly could be considered heavenly fortune.

"I didn't expect you to request such a meeting during the banquet," said Young Family Head Gongya Jeong.

She had no choice but to serve as acting family head. That was why she sat at the head of the spacious room with the family elders flanking her on either side.

As the orthodox successor of the Gongya martial lineage, she bore the responsibility of responding to the distinguished guests before her.

Before her sat the various Shanxi aristocrats, each surrounded by extraordinary energy waves.

The pavilion by the lake was a secluded villa belonging to the Gongya family.

It stood some distance from the main estate, at the request of aristocrats concerned about the perception range of Desolate Fortress's black-ranks. They claimed to have urgent matters to discuss.

"We can't waste time meaninglessly, can we?" replied a person of extremely large build, smiling with cunning.

Mountain-Crushing Cloud-Cutter Hui Ho-ryang.

She was a woman with a large blade strapped to her back.

Seemingly unaffected by the peak of winter's cold, she wore only a hemp garment, revealing the powerful muscles of both arms.

With translucent energy waves rippling through every tendon, she was worthy of her title as the family head of Shanxi's renowned Great Hui Blade Family.

Even her gaze, boring into Gongya Jeong's eyes, was extraordinary.

"Family Head Hui is right. Why should we remain when the principal figures of the banquet have vanished?"

Seated beside Hui Ho-ryang were three people in identical attire.

They were sword practitioners with long limbs—triplets.

They were the masters of the Zhijiao Martial Academy. After the deaths of the Gao Sword Sect Leader, Shou Yue Sect Leader, and Azure Sky Clan Leader at the hands of Zhuge Clan Leader and Slaughter Chivalry, they had more or less assumed leadership of the aristocrat group.

Their fierce gazes and the formless sword energy they emitted without even drawing their blades made this inevitable.

Both sides were more than Gongya Jeong could handle.

When her father drew his sword to confront Zhuge Clan Leader, the family elders had formed elements of the sword formation to assist the family head.

That was why those seated to her left and right now wore darkened complexions.

'This is my burden to bear,' thought the Young Family Head.

It was then that one of the Zhijiao Martial Academy masters spoke.

"Establish a connection with Radiant Demon Wing Leader. Although he doesn't treat the Shanxi aristocrats as worth his notice, regardless of that, we must request to learn from him."

"Are you referring to a martial arts duel?"

"Precisely."

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader can barely move. The same goes for Blood Guardian Squad Leader and Divine Sword Squad Leader. It would be better for you to visit Xiangyang, where Desolate Fortress's main fortress is located, after spring has passed."

Gongya Jeong said with narrowed eyes.

Immediately after, the hem of her red robe rose dramatically. She had risen from her seat.

The oppressive atmosphere in the room wavered for a moment. This was not proper conduct in the presence of so many powerful masters.

"Since you are not the sort to leave when asked, we will depart instead. If you wish to enjoy the banquet, come to the main house."

Thud!

With that, Gongya Jeong led eight family members out.

Energy waves of lightness skill spread in every direction from her toes. She seemed ready to shatter the firmly constructed threshold.

Crash!

And indeed she did. Their rushing figures were closer to flight than retreat.

How would martial artists respond to such an unexpected escape? Powerful predators would naturally give chase first.

Among the aristocrats who rose from their seats in confusion, Hui Ho-ryang frowned.

"Their behavior is suspicious. We didn't even threaten them with blades..."

"Are they trying to make innocent people into criminals?!"

"They're trying to frame us with false accusations before the officials and Desolate Fortress!"

Two of the Zhijiao Martial Academy masters stamped the ground, launching themselves through the threshold Gongya Jeong had shattered.

Their lightness skill pursuit of the Gongya family grew increasingly urgent. The room was instantly demolished by the powerful energy waves propelling them.

Boom boom boom boom!

A chase had formed.

Eight red robes of the Gongya family raced ahead across the ground, while dozens of Shanxi aristocrats rapidly closed the distance with accelerating lightness skills.

All were leaders of their respective families. Shouts commanding them to stop swept across the terrain.

It was then.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

The roaring sound came from the side. An explosive noise no human body could produce.

The aristocrats' complexions changed as they glanced sideways. They had spotted dozens of black spheres hurtling through the void.

Anyone with even a passing knowledge would recognize them. The infamous thunderclap bombs of the martial world.

"Disciples, continue the pursuit! The Gongya ruffians seem to be trying to deceive the Deputy Commander!"

"You too, follow Family Head Hui's orders!"

Four figures soared toward the cluster of thunderclap bombs. Hui Ho-ryang and the Zhijiao Martial Academy masters.

They were masters who had competed for dominance in the Shanxi martial world, which boasted a size comparable to Shandong under the Hwangbo family's control.

All were close to supreme masters. They were capable of applying the marvel of harmonious state to deflect the incoming projectiles away from the bombs.

Then, suddenly.

The eyes of the four masters who were gently extending their swords toward the thunderclap bombs went wide. The same was true for the aristocrats crossing behind them.

Whoosh!

The wilderness was bathed in a light resembling dawn.

From where had he leaped? A figure in black robes grew rapidly larger, turning everything around him pale.

With a dim radiance, he rose in a straight line, approaching with the overwhelming presence of a constellation descending.

Radiant Demon Wing Leader Jeong Yeon-shin had merged with the cluster of thunderclap bombs.

From his outstretched hand, an incredibly powerful current created a dragon-like whirlwind.

The energy waves, extending adhesion and dispersion forces in streams, instantly took control of dozens of thunderclap bombs, and in that moment, Desolate Fortress's Myriad Heavens Flower Rain—composed of imperial swords—revealed itself.

Woong—

The shower of thunderclap bombs, rather than hidden weapons, transformed into unpredictable variations infused with peerless secret techniques. The aristocrats' swords sliced through nothing but empty air.

"Radiant Demon Wing Leader!"

"Surely not...!"

Their wails, rapidly expelled through oral techniques, were soon buried beneath the ensuing symphony of explosions.

The pitch-black Myriad Heavens Flower Rain left no witnesses.

Boom boom boom boom boom boom boom BOOM—!
